---
title: "Matthew Martin"
pagetitle: "About | Matthew Martin"
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="va-trestles">
  <div class="va-trestles-left">
    <div class="va-portrait">
      <img src="imgs/headshot.jpg" alt="Matthew Martin">
    </div>
    <div class="va-namecard">
      <div class="name">Matthew Martin</div>
      <div class="va-appointment">
        <div class="role">Assistant Professor</div>
        <div class="org">Department of Political Science</div>
        <div class="org">University of South Carolina</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="va-bio">
    <p class="lead">My name is Matt Martin (he/him), and I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Political Science at the <a class="org" href="https://sc.edu/study/colleges_schools/artsandsciences/political_science/index.php">University of South Carolina</a>. I received my PhD in Government from the University of Texas at Austin, specializing in public law and comparative politics. Previously, I was a postdoctoral researcher at the <a class="org" href="https://comparativeconstitutionsproject.org/">Comparative Constitutions Project</a>.</p>

    <p>My research examines constitution-making processes, focusing on mechanisms of public consultation. My dissertation analyzes how and why constitutional drafters engage public consultation&mdash;and what they do with the input they receive&mdash;through comparative case studies of Chile and Cuba. I combine large-N analyses and natural language processing with elite interviews and qualitative case research.</p>

    <p>My work has appeared in <a href="https://doi.org/10.1177/08944393261443513">Social Science Computer Review</a>, <a href="https://doi.org/10.5129/001041526X17731675177333">Comparative Politics</a>, <a href="https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/10.1086/739835">Polity</a>, <a href="https://revistapolitica.uchile.cl/index.php/RP/article/view/81160">Pol&iacute;tica</a>, the <a href="https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/journal-of-law-and-courts/article/elite-fractures-public-capture-the-strategic-use-of-public-consultation-in-global-constitutionmaking/A6BBCC7D2A7B5744D7766ABB4293595D">Journal of Law and Courts</a>, and <a href="https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0295396">PLOS ONE</a>. My dissertation research was supported by a Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grant from the American Political Science Association. I am also an affiliate of the <a class="org" href="https://liberalarts.utexas.edu/lawanddemocracy/">Center for Law and Democracy</a> at UT Austin.</p>

    <p>Prior to graduate school, I worked as a legal assistant in immigration law. I remain committed to migrant justice through mutual aid work supporting trans and gender non-conforming asylum seekers. Outside of work, I play guitar and produce <a href="https://soundcloud.com/meridiem-beats">music</a>&mdash;mostly lofi hip-hop.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```
